$comment: Slice 1 of 3 in the Feature-Prune sprint. Surgical removals of small-blast-radius features Kevin doesn't use. No UI restructuring. Ship phase by phase.

context.test_corpus: 1135 / 120 (1023 client + 111 functions + 1 invariant added in Arch-Cleanup)
context.why: Kevin reviewed a 132-feature inventory and named these as not-used / not-wanted. Cut completely per his 'Not needed = cut completely' rule. This slice is the small-radius set; meet/live-ops removal is in slice 2; attendance group-first enhancement is slice 3.

edit_radius: src/services/aggregations.ts (only if it pre-aggregates a 7-day window)
app/_layout.tsx (drop swimmer/medical route registration if present)
src/types/firestore.types.ts (drop medical fields from Swimmer type)
src/services/swimmers.ts (or wherever the type is read/written)
src/services/__tests__/* (any test fixtures setting medical fields)
test/fixtures/coach/builders.ts (drop medical builder fields if present)
app/_layout.tsx (drop practice-plan/[id] Stack.Screen + any deep-link config)
Anywhere that does router.push('/practice-plan/...') or Linking config (grep first)
PHASE 4 (AI practice generator removal — full stack):
app/(tabs)/practice.tsx line 279 — drop AI button entry
app/practice/library.tsx line 118 — drop aiBtn TouchableOpacity + styles
app/_layout.tsx line 570 — drop Stack.Screen for practice/ai-generate
src/services/docExport.ts — VERIFY only that generatePracticePlanDoc has no aiPractice dependency; do not delete (still used for docx export of plain plans)
read_first: .codex/FULL_APP_OVERVIEW.md (sections B, C, D, F to confirm scope)
docs/process/sim-engine-quality-gates.md (only if any phase touches simulation/RNG — none expected)
deliverables: PHASE 1: Recent PRs feed gone from dashboard data hook + JSX. sparkData window expanded from 7 to 30 days. Layout adjusted so 30 dots are readable on a phone (consider thinner bars / hidden labels except for first/today/midpoints — Codex's call). Tests updated.

PHASE 2: Medical button gone from swimmer profile. /swimmer/medical route deleted. Medical fields gone from Swimmer type. Existing Firestore documents are NOT modified — old medical fields stay on disk untouched (no migration, no delete writes); the type just stops reading/writing them. Tests pass without medical fixtures.
PHASE 3: app/practice-plan/[id].tsx deleted. No remaining router.push to that route. Deep-link config (if it referenced practice-plan) cleaned up. App still launches and tests pass.

PHASE 4: AI practice generator route + UI entries + client service + Cloud Function + tests all deleted. functions/src/index.ts no longer exports generatePractice. functions build still clean. Practice tab no longer shows an AI button. functions tests still 110/17 (was 111/18, minus the deleted generatePractice suite).
Each phase = one commit. Conventional commit format. Phase order is P1 → P2 → P3 → P4 (smallest blast radius first).
acceptance_criteria: Working tree clean after each commit.

All existing tests still pass after each phase. New corpus targets — P1: 1135/120, P2: 1135/120 (test count may shift if medical-specific tests deleted; surface in regression_summary), P3: same, P4: client suites unchanged minus aiPractice.test.ts; functions suites = 17 (was 18) and tests = 110 (was 111) assuming generatePractice.test.ts had 1 suite.
npm run lint:errors → 0 errors. Pre-existing 181 warnings may decrease as dead code is removed; do not introduce new warnings.
(cd functions && npm run build) clean — sync:functions-shared:verify still passes (notification-rules untouched).
No dead exports — npm run quality:dead-code shows nothing introduced by this slice.
Spark chart still renders with 30 data points; layout doesn't break on common widths (verify on iPhone 14 simulator size or check with snapshot if one exists).
Deep-link tests (if any) updated — search test/ for practice-plan refs.
out_of_scope: Meet creation / entries / relays / live-ops removal (slice 2).
Migrating existing Firestore docs to drop medical fields — leave them on disk; the app just stops reading them.

Touching the AI knowledge base (src/utils/aiKnowledge or similar) — it may still be used by audio observation extraction. Confirm before touching; in scope only if exclusively used by generatePractice.
Refactoring docExport.ts beyond removing AI-only paths.
Touching simulation, save/load, schema, RNG — none of these phases require it. If a deletion forces a schema change, halt per stop_conditions.
stop_conditions: A deletion would require a Firestore schema migration — halt and document.
An existing baseline test fails after edits — apply baseline_update_protocol.
AI knowledge base (data/ai-knowledge or src/utils/aiKnowledge) turns out to have non-AI-practice consumers — keep it; only drop generatePractice-specific code.
docExport.generatePracticePlanDoc has hidden coupling to aiPractice — halt; partial-delete is unsafe.
Spark expansion from 7 to 30 days needs a new aggregation collection / Cloud Function — halt; original spec was UI-window-only.
Removing the medical screen breaks parent-portal callable function tests in a way that can't be fixed by dropping the field — halt.
baseline_update_protocol: Run the existing corpus first to establish baseline.
If output changed, produce a human-readable diff (file, test name, old assertion, new assertion, reason).
Classify the change: intended (deletion-induced) vs. unintended.
Update expected outputs only when the change is intended and documented in this slice.
Note the new corpus totals in final_response_required_format.regression_summary.

final_response_required_format.files_changed: Every file touched, grouped by phase.
final_response_required_format.verification: Exact commands run and pass/fail per phase.
final_response_required_format.regression_summary: Test corpus deltas: client (was 1023/102) → ?, functions (was 111/18) → ?, combined → ?
final_response_required_format.risks: Anything Codex couldn't fully verify (dead-code coverage, deep-link config edge cases, etc.).
final_response_required_format.handoff: Confirm slice 2 (meet refactor) and slice 3 (attendance group-first) are unblocked, or surface any newly-discovered coupling.
